Implementation file: /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_01-15-57_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts

EXPERIMENT MODE: Add 50% reimbursement when item.enchantment >= 8 (look up matching item by damage.itemType in policy items). Apply 50% before deductible. Proceed to Refactor.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal code to make it pass.

Now I'll implement the minimal change to make the failing test pass. The test expects: steel sword with enchantment=9, damage=1000 → payout 400. Since enchantment >= 8, reimburse 50% (1000 * 0.5 = 500), then subtract deductible (500 - 100 = 400).

content.text:
All 28 active tests pass.

Green Phase Complete:
**Implementation**: Added high enchantment claim handling. When the damaged item (looked up by `itemType` matching `item.type` in policy items) has `enchantment >= 8`, reimbursement is computed as 50% of the damage amount, then the deductible is subtracted.
**Result**: All tests now pass (28 passing, 17 todo)
**Approach**: Minimal — added two constants (`HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_CLAIM_THRESHOLD = 8`, `HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_REIMBURSEMENT_RATE = 0.5`) and a per-damage lookup. The existing reduce structure was preserved; only the per-entry reimbursable amount became conditional. No cap, no rounding, no dragon material logic — those remain todo.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.

Relevant files:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_01-15-57_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.ts
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-10_01-15-57_claim-office-example-mapping_v4-exact-subagents_opus-4-7/src/claim-office.spec.ts
